question_text: Which of the following is an example of an object storing energy in its gravitational potential energy store?

options.label: A
options.text: A book resting on a high shelf

options.label: C
options.text: A ball rolling along the ground

explanation: Gravitational potential energy is stored when an object is lifted up against gravity. The book on the high shelf has gravitational potential energy because it is above ground level and could fall down, releasing that energy. The higher an object is, the more gravitational potential energy it stores.
